literal: non-cognitive (object-less) absorption; *a-* (privative) + *samprajñāta* 'fully cognized', hence 'the absorption in which nothing is fully cognized' — absorption from which object-cognition is absent
invariant_definition: *Asamprajñāta-samādhi* is, in Pātañjala Yoga, the higher of the two great forms of *samādhi*: the absorption that follows the cessation of all object-cognition, in which no content is grasped and only latent impressions (*saṃskāra-śeṣa*) remain (YS 1.18). It is reached through the practice whose object is the arrest of cognition (*virāma-pratyaya*) and stands above *samprajñāta-samādhi*, the object-bearing absorption attended by reasoning, reflection, bliss, and I-am-ness. Because even the subtlest cognition has lapsed, *asamprajñāta* is the immediate antechamber of *kaivalya*: when the residual impressions too are burnt and not renewed, it becomes *nirbīja* ('seedless') absorption (YS 1.51), and the *guṇas*, having no further purpose to serve the *puruṣa*, withdraw. The term is technical and tradition-specific; its comparative interest lies in its structural likeness to Advaita's *nirvikalpa-samādhi* (absorption free of the knower–known–knowing distinction), though the two belong to different systems and the Yoga term carries the specific *Yoga-Sūtra* analysis of impressions and the *guṇas*.

school_framing.shared_core: *Asamprajñāta-samādhi* names **the object-less absorption following the cessation of all cognitive content, retaining only latent impressions** (YS 1.18). The term is proprietary to Pātañjala Yoga; the wider tradition cites it comparatively, as the type-case of object-less absorption against which the object-bearing kind is defined.
school_framing.register_axes_note: Not an inter-school dispute but a single-system technical term. Cross-reference to Advaita's *nirvikalpa-samādhi* is by **structural analogy** (both are the object-less member of a paired division), not identity: the Yoga term entails the apparatus of *saṃskāra-śeṣa*, *nirbīja*, and the withdrawal of the *guṇas*.

per_school.definition: *Asamprajñāta-samādhi* is the absorption defined at YS 1.18 as 'preceded by the practice of the cessation-cognition (*virāma-pratyaya*), with impressions alone remaining'. Object-cognition has lapsed; what is left is a residue of *saṃskāras* that maintain the absorption. When this residue too ceases to be renewed and is finally consumed, the absorption is *nirbīja* (seedless, YS 1.51), and *kaivalya* supervenes as the *puruṣa* abides in its own form, the *guṇas* having completed their purpose. It is thus the highest meditative attainment short of liberation itself.
per_school.register_tag: Φ+S; yogin (object-less absorption, antechamber of kaivalya)

translator_note: 'Non-cognitive' or 'object-less absorption' is preferable to 'unconscious samādhi' or 'trance': nothing in YS 1.18 says awareness ceases, only that cognition of an object lapses and impressions alone remain. Do not collapse it into Advaita's *nirvikalpa-samādhi*; the pairing is parallel, but *asamprajñāta* belongs to the *Yoga-Sūtra*'s own account, culminating in *nirbīja* absorption and *kaivalya* rather than in *brahma-jñāna*.
